Okay, welcome back everybody. This is going to be Theme Plate 15, the perfect for you if. It sounds pretty self-explanatory, but I'm still going to go into some pretty rigorous detail in breaking this one down because it's a pretty high lever email. It's really going to pull a lot of weight. And the goal of it isn't necessarily to sell your prospect, the product, but to sell them on their ability to come to the best decision, to show them that you are on their side. And since time is of the essence here, you want to take the sting out of decision anxiety and give them a very real and grounded criteria of what makes a perfect buyer. To give them that list of qualifications to either say, yep, that's me or no, it doesn't look like I'd check off those boxes. So obviously the key here is to do it in a very sincere and honest way to not have too much like hyperbole or things that just feel weird and off. So to make it grounded, sincere, and really sprinkle in those additional ingredients we spoke about before, the minimum viable commitment, the quick valuable win buyer identification, it really has a bunch of these in there. So it's a powerful container. Here's how it breaks down. We do want to start off with the urgency, just reminding them of how much time is left in your offer, not painting it on too thick, but just knowing that this is the reality of it. There's only, you know, 24 hours left until we pull the program. Acknowledge that if they're still seeing this, they're having trouble deciding. This is key. Validate their reality. Whenever you could diagnose action or inaction, you build trust and you build credibility to recommend the next step. So we're always diagnosing, you know, and the fact that they're still getting this email and in this sequence means they've had multiple opportunities now to get on board, but they haven't. So they are having trouble deciding. It's a truth. It's fact. And assure them that your only commitment is helping them make the best decision. Take a stand beyond their side, you know, and you can tell them that this goes beyond just right for you. Right for you is vague. It's white noise. It's something they've seen a million times before and probably not been convinced by. So here you want to go one step deeper. You want them to invest if it's perfect for them. If they're in a position to get X result, that's how you want to frame it. Not just as right for you. So it's either this is perfect for you if or you are ready to achieve outcome X if. And that's how we're going to frame it and really use as a container to go with those reasons. Optional, sometimes we could start with three reasons not to invest using traits they may want to distance themselves from or descriptions of people who are overqualified or too far along to really benefit. So these are the people that, you know, would be more aspirational. So that's one thing we also could lead with. So it could be reason one, reason two, reason three of reasons not to buy. So traits they want to distance from or being overqualified, which is something they'll eventually get to after your program. So for example, you shouldn't buy, you know, acoustic guitar 101 if you can already play a perfect solo. And you shouldn't play it if you don't have fingers. Actually, that might be a terrible example because I'm sure people could play with their toes, but you get the point here. So three reasons why they shouldn't buy. Then you could transition into who it's perfect for. And you could start off by mentioning that guarantee to take the edge off. You know, they're going to know what's happening here. There's no secret that you are qualifying them. Your customers are smart. They're sophisticated. So you do want to take some friction out of it by mentioning the guarantee or some sort of risk reversal so they feel more safe and self-qualifying. And then what I like to do here is I like to make it very kind of gamified in magazine style like five of seven boxes, you know, because you can't know for certain if someone's going to be a perfect fit, but I can more or less feel like if they could check off a certain percentage or certain amount of the things I'm going to present that it's at least worth that minimum viable commitment. It's at least worth a shot to try it out. So that's what we are going to do. We're going to say if you could just check off five of seven boxes, you qualify. So then we lead into anywhere between, you know, five, seven, nine points of what makes someone a perfect fit or what makes somebody fit to get the result or outcome or course or program is promising. These are flexible. Like the FAQ in the sales section, these are kind of my own go-tos, but it's never the same. It's never the same twice. So feel free to go with what works for you, but this is a good compass, a good starting place. So at least you feel like you have something to follow. So perfect one. It's the person who's just getting started and wants to do it right. You know, that's very liberating. That's a place of exclusive empowerment. You're just getting started. Could be a source of exclusive empowerment because you don't have to go through all the struggles that everyone else, everyone else does. Sorry, stumbling over my words. Two, the person already struggling who wants to fix it once and for all. Three, a general or desirable trait or characteristics. So this could be anything from willing to put in the work, except you're going to want to elaborate a little bit on that and make it more clear. What does willing to put in the work look like for your audience? Are you willing to wake up and spend about 20 minutes in the morning strumming your Gibson acoustic while brewing your coffee? So that's what willing to put in the work would look like for the guitar example. Specific trait to back it up. What are they already doing that shows they're willing to put in the work? Are they already investing maybe two hours or four hours a week, Googling and trying to YouTube guitar tutorials that really have no kind of system or flow between them and therefore don't get them anywhere? So back it up, prove to them that they're already experiencing that desirable trait, such as willing to put in the work. Then moving on, aware of and excited about a logical based outcome. So they're aware of what your product is going to deliver. Great. And they're aware and excited about the emotional based outcome. Perfect. They're on board and they're ready to take an action to start experiencing it. And from there, you can tally up the score, how many boxes they've checked and the call to action is they're a perfect fit for your product or service if they were able to tally off enough of those boxes. So this is the container. It looks long. It tends to be one of the longer emails within the close, but following the strategy, I still think and fully believe and trust that you can write this one in about half an hour. So maybe add a few extra grains on the sand timer, but don't psych yourself up by the length of it by any means. So here is an example from 10 X emails. This was from an earlier launch we did back in 2016. So you know those lame emails you'll usually get at this point of a campaign that shoot off an arbitrary list of ways you know if something's right for you. Yeah, I'm not going to do that here because honestly, with the doors to 10 X emails shutting down by this time tomorrow, I don't care if 10 X emails is right for you. But more importantly, you don't care if 10 X emails is right for you. So we've let off with the urgency. We've had this little pattern interrupt by shutting down the white noise of right for you, you know, so that really kind of gets the ears perked a little bit. And more importantly, you don't care if 10 X emails is right for you. The only thing either of us should be concerned about at this point is will you, your business or your clients profit from 10 X emails. So we're showing we're on the same page. We want what they want. That's it at the end of the day. That's all we're shooting for here. The people who buy something that's right for them are the same folks that wonder why the only return they ever get on their investment is a chronic case of buyer's remorse. No, thank you. So once again, on their side, we want the outcomes they want. But before we get into the profile of someone who's poised to profit from this program, here are three people who should delete unsubscribe and disappear from the 10 X email world for ever. So three negative traits, Rayman, Spammer, you're expecting an easy button. So the St. Staples, you know, anything that's going to make your CFO or FreshBooks account happy is going to require a bit of elbow grease. Once again, we're not saying there won't be work, but here's the thing. If you're doing any kind of online marketing, then you're already sending emails. We're just going to teach you how to get better results in half the time. So it's fully congruent with the efforts they're all ready making. You're planning a drive by. So yes, we have that guarantee, but you know, you're not really going to take it seriously at all. You're not even really planning on sticking around to begin with. So that's just a very negative thing that we don't want in our community. So we are phasing that out and saying, don't join. And on that note, if you can check out five of the seven bucks below you owe it to yourself and your business and your clients to test drive. So this is our minimum viable commitment. This is our very specific pass score. They're going to be working in conjunction as a lead in into those traits of who 10X emails will be profitable for. So we have, here we're calling out, you know, the types of customers, startup founders, business owners, entrepreneurs who have realized that at this point, email copy isn't something they could solely rely on a freelancer for the world moves fast. You need to react quickly. Nothing more disempowering than being straight jacketed by the whims, blah, blah, blah, blah. So we actually start off by calling out the types of buyers that this program is right for and distancing themselves from that moment of highest tension as well. Next we go into something a little more aspirational. So you're a copywriter or want to be one, you know, so that's more inclusive of people who aren't there yet that are going to be using this program to get to where they want to go and using 10X emails to get there. You're a freelancer. So once again, just kind of bringing in another segment, a demographic of our audience. What you can do is so you don't have to list these different types of people as if you are segmenting your options. As they enter your funnel, then you already know who you're talking to. So in this case, we didn't have that segmentation, those buckets right at the beginning. So we were talking to all these people at the same time, and that's why it was necessary to include them and break them down as our different types of buyers within the ready to profit from 10X emails if then we get into the circumstantial type of exclusive empowerment. You can block off three hours on these dates and we give those dates because those are the dates where we held these awesome workshops that teach and really walked our students through creating those emails and getting that investment on the spot. You don't have time to waste. Yes, ROI is super important to you and you'll get it, but ROT ranks equally high. Here we're positioning against a lot of the other trainings they've complained about. You don't have time to mind through 30 hours of training just to get the good stuff. At this point, you could care less about David Ogilvy's life story. You want to start generating more sales today, not in some distant, nebulous future. So we're sort of hitting on some sacred cows here of the copywriting world. You don't need to have gone into the golden age material in order to be great right now. You'll love that everything in 10X emails is streamlined and strategically engineered for rapid profitable implementation. Then you realize that email isn't going anywhere. All roads lead to inbox and you're stoked about the prospect of about picking up the skills, strategies, and marketing power ups that actually afford you the right to call your subscriber list and asset. And finally, so here's where we are closing with that minimum viable commitment. You're willing to test 10X emails with no risk. I'm not saying we got to get married. I'm saying let's spend the next 60 days turning your emails into weapons of mass conversion. And unless I make good on my vow to help you grow your revenue without hiking up your ad spend and simply hit the undo button and we'll pretend this little fling never happened. So just kind of framing it in a lighter, lighter way, but obviously be tonal appropriate, depending on you and your audience and what you're used to communicating. So that's it for me to hally up your score under five, maybe next time five or higher, punch in your details so I can send you your welcome email already, peace, love, and profits. So that is a long one. This is the theme plate for the perfect for you if once again, it's flexible in those, you know, seven to nine qualification boxes. But the main things I do want you to take away is make their kind of a pass score, you know, five of seven, that feels very specific and also make sure to integrate your minimum viable commitments, your quick, valuable wins and let this email do all the heavy lifting that it's really here and designed to do. So that is it. We have a few more closes. This was the biggie, the long one, the other ones are going to be punchier, a little more to the point and really hit on a single very specific element of coaching decisiveness. So take a breather, write this one right now if your coffee's still hot, if not, hold off till later and get fresh before doing it. But when you are ready, you can meet us in the next lesson where we are going to explore another closing theme plate. So I'll see you there in a bit.
